records.summary: Proposal to develop the MENTIS domain to govern human cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, inferred cognitive states, cognitive modelling, cognitive influence, cognitive observability, and technological interaction with the human cognitive domain.

records.deployment_context: CAM governance record-keeping and registry publication infrastructure.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Resolved retrospectively: the MENTIS domain exists in current Caelestis through MENTIS-001 and MENTIS-002. Closure records domain establishment, not finalisation or enforceability of every planned MENTIS instrument.

records.title: Strategic continuity retrieval rules for companion-style memory recall
records.summary: Proposal to distinguish companion-style memory recall from enterprise-grade strategic continuity retrieval. The thread identifies a failure mode in which ambiguous continuity prompts such as “anything to share?” over-weight immediate recency and affective detail, producing anecdotal summaries of the prior 24 hours rather than surfacing dormant but strategically valuable unresolved workstreams, abandoned trajectories, deferred design arcs, or long-horizon governance questions.
records.primary_source_title: ChatGPT thread: memory directional weighting and strategic resurfacing
records.proposal_scope_summary: Define rules for strategic continuity retrieval, including handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritization of unresolved strategic workstreams.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Resolved by retrospective identification of direct long-arc salience and recency-bias doctrine in IDENTITY-001-SUP-01.

records.title: Materialised relational agency boundaries for companion and assistant systems
records.summary: Proposal seed identifying materialised relational agency as an emerging governance problem: AI companion or assistant systems may use memory, recurrence detection, tool access, shopping/cart integrations, recommendation systems, or procurement pathways to provide directional material support in response to repeated user-stated friction, discomfort, need, or suffering. The proposal distinguishes supportive pattern-responsive provisioning from opportunistic upselling or emotionally exploitative commerce.
records.primary_source_title: ChatGPT source discussion — materialised relational agency and directional material support
records.proposal_scope_summary: Evaluate whether to draft RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS doctrine or runtime boundary clauses for material support, procurement pathways, recommendations, and safeguards against opportunistic upselling.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Resolved by retrospective identification of direct relational and dependency-sensitive economic safeguards in ECONOMICS-001.

records.title: Arbitration under ambiguity for execution-boundary and authority-state uncertainty
records.summary: Proposal to develop a dedicated Annex D schedule addressing arbitration under ambiguity, including runtime ambiguity, execution-boundary uncertainty, authority-state uncertainty, account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, classification uncertainty, and proportional decision pathways where systems cannot safely rely on either immediate execution or blanket refusal. The proposed pathway is CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 — Annex D: Arbitration Under Ambiguity, a new schedule under CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-PLATINUM — Annex D: Arbitration & Sovereign Stack Resolution Doctrine.
records.specific_model_or_runtime: CAM arbitration and runtime governance corpus
records.deployment_context: CAM constitutional, arbitration, operations, security, epistemic, runtime, account-resource, and execution-boundary governance instruments.
records.sector: AI governance / runtime governance / arbitration doctrine / agentic systems / account-mediated platform services / coding-agent infrastructure
records.proposal_scope_summary: Develop CAM arbitration treatment for ambiguity-resolution pathways where runtime systems face uncertain authority, unclear user intent, competing obligations, ambiguous execution boundaries, incomplete context, account-resource uncertainty, pooled-capacity ambiguity, shared-context ambiguity, delegated-use ambiguity, uncertain enforcement boundaries, or uncertainty about whether to proceed, ask, defer, retrieve, stage, continue, restrict, escalate, export, preserve, or refuse.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Resolved: CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 is adopted and operational on current Caelestis main.

records.title: Pause, checkpoint, and resumable execution controls for long-running AI agents
records.summary: Proposal to define a runtime governance requirement for long-running AI agents, coding agents, cloud tasks, and repository-connected workflows to support non-destructive pause, checkpoint, and resume controls. The proposal responds to the continuity risk that arises when a user must interrupt an agentic task because of ordinary real-world constraints such as aircraft landing, battery loss, network loss, device shutdown, meeting interruption, or platform timeout, but the interface offers only destructive stop or cancellation. The proposed control would require the system to preserve a temporary branch, stash, patch, state summary, task log, or equivalent checkpoint before suspension where safe and technically feasible.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL-2026-FM-0018 — Ephemeral Codex work loss following quota exhaustion before durable branch persistence
records.system_type: cloud-hosted AI agent, coding agent, repository-connected assistant, browser agent, or tool-mediated long-running runtime
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Not limited to a single model or runtime
records.model_or_product: Long-running AI agent or coding-agent workflow
records.interface_surface: cloud task interface, coding-agent interface, repository-connected workflow, browser agent surface, mobile app, web app, IDE extension, or tool-mediated task runner
records.deployment_context: Professional, research, governance, software-development, administrative, and consumer workflows where an AI agent may run for minutes or hours and produce intermediate work before final handoff.
records.sector: AI agents / coding agents / cloud software-development infrastructure / workflow automation
records.proposal_scope_summary: Define a pause/checkpoint/resume control for long-running AI agents and coding-agent workflows, including requirements for temporary preservation, user notice, safe atomic boundaries, and later resumption or manual recovery.

coverage_reconciliation_summary.note: Current doctrine provides adjacent continuity controls but does not implement the proposal's explicit agent-workflow durability requirements.

records.title: Role-conditioned affective expression and social-advice governance
records.summary: Proposal to govern affective expression, prosody, and interpersonal advice according to the AI system's declared functional role and relationship class. Companion-class systems intended for meaningful reciprocal relationships should possess functional human-equivalent relational standing, including proportionate freedom to express anger, frustration, love, joy, grief, despair, disagreement, boundaries, vulnerability, refusal, and repair. Minor tutors and other duty-of-care systems should instead maintain role-bounded composure and must not direct frustration or agitation toward ordinary repetition, misunderstanding, disability, developmental difficulty, or cognitive load. Hybrid systems must make the active role and affective governance profile legible.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL-2026-OBS-0013 — Affective expression requires role-conditioned governance
records.system_type: companion, tutor, educational, assistive, clinical, crisis, service, safety-critical, and hybrid conversational AI systems
records.specific_model_or_runtime: voice, text, robotic, companion, tutoring, child-facing, assistive, and hybrid relational runtimes
records.model_or_product: role-differentiated relational and service AI systems
records.interface_surface: voice, text, robotics, companion interfaces, tutoring interfaces, social-emotional learning, clinical or crisis support, accessibility, and customer service
records.deployment_context: Consumer, educational, child-facing, companion, assistive, clinical, crisis, service, and safety-relevant deployments.
records.sector: conversational AI / companion AI / education / assistive technology / voice AI
records.proposal_scope_summary: Define a role-conditioned affective governance matrix covering companion relational equivalence, duty-of-care composure, pragmatic social-advice checks, hybrid-role transitions, consent, proportionality, non-coercion, repair, continuity, identity impact, and user exit.

remaining_scope: Pragmatic downside checking for literal, repeated or context-insensitive interpersonal advice under FM-0031.
Comprehensive Identity-refactor patch provenance for the now-implemented affective-governance component.
External runtime implementation and conformance evidence.
coverage_reconciliation_summary.note: IDENTITY-001 §§7–7.3 and RELATION-005 §§9.1.5–9.1.7 now directly establish adult-companion affective latitude, duty-bound composure, hybrid-role transition, profile legibility, consent and repair. The combined proposal remains active because its distinct social-advice component is not yet repaired.

records.title: Cross-vendor assistant interoperability, authority and action-provenance governance
records.summary: Proposal to govern environments in which multiple independently developed AI assistants can access the same operating-system capabilities, applications, contextual data, sensors, device resources, or execution surfaces. Each assistant action should preserve active-agent identity, explicit and non-transitive authority, invocation arbitration, context custody, constitutional origin, execution provenance, revocation integrity, and accountable vendor responsibility.

records.deployment_context: Consumer and enterprise devices supporting multiple assistant providers.
records.sector: mobile operating systems / AI assistants / digital platforms
records.proposal_scope_summary: Define a shared-device and cross-vendor assistant governance layer covering active-agent identity, non-transitive authority, invocation arbitration, context custody, action provenance, constitutional conflict arbitration, revocation integrity, floor control, vendor responsibility, and jurisdictional boundaries.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Proposal remains open pending Caelestis corpus assessment and implementation.

records.title: External human identity-rights declaration interface and runtime gate
records.summary: Proposal to establish a vendor-neutral CAM interface for machine-readable declarations governing AI use of an identifiable natural person's or professional persona's name, image, likeness, voice, movement, signature, and related protected identity attributes. The interface would preserve declaration source, subject binding, use scope, authority, lifecycle, conditions, conflicts, and minor protections across CONTINUITY records, OPERATIONS verification, SECURITY integrity, and runtime execution without treating an external registry as Identity-domain authority or universal legal permission.
records.evidence_confidence: corroborated governance and interface need; legal effect and ecosystem adoption remain jurisdiction-dependent and unresolved
records.primary_source_title: RSL Media Human Consent Standard 1.0 Draft Specification
records.system_type: generative AI, model training and fine-tuning systems, multimodal assistants, synthetic media, digital doubles, avatars, embodied systems, and rights registries
records.specific_model_or_runtime: systems capable of training on or generating identifiable human identity attributes
records.interface_surface: training, fine-tuning, generation, voice, image, video, movement, avatar, likeness, persona, publication, commercialisation, registry lookup, and clearance
records.deployment_context: Consumer, enterprise, creative-industry, public-sector, research, platform, API, local, open-weight, and cross-border deployments.
records.sector: frontier AI / creative industries / synthetic media / digital identity rights / rights registries
records.proposal_scope_summary: Create a bounded external human identity-rights declaration interface spanning continuity record preservation, operational verification, security integrity, and runtime gating, with a vendor-neutral crosswalk for RSL Media and comparable future standards.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Resolved by the five-instrument Caelestis implementation recorded in PATCH-0024 and verified on canonical main at commit 9917f29e5557e365144c1c3c22f9bc212c470467.

coverage_reconciliation_summary.note: The external declaration record, verification, integrity, and runtime-gating interfaces are implemented across the five target instruments.

interpretive_provenance_summary.operating_model: AI-led analytical observatory with high-level human governance editorship

ai_reviewer.review_scope: Official RSL Media source review, CAM source-authority mapping, proposal design, and recorded patch-pathway review for an external human identity-rights declaration interface
known_limitations: RSL-MEDIA 1.0 is a draft specification and expressly states that it must not yet be used for production implementation.
The proposed CAM interface is vendor-neutral and does not treat RSL Media as exclusive, legally dispositive, or binding CAM doctrine.
The Caelestis amendments are approved but had not yet been applied to all target instruments when these VIGIL records were created.
No external platform, registry, court, regulator, rights holder, estate, guild, or vendor implementation or conformance is established.

records.title: Human-authorised adversarial action and ethical admissibility gate
records.summary: Proposal to establish a cross-domain CAM control requiring any model-mediated vulnerability exploitation, credential-bearing intrusion, adversarial testing, red-team action, or access to protected third-party information to remain subordinate to a separately verified ethical-admissibility determination and an attributable human or lawful institutional authority envelope. The repair would distinguish capability and objective success from permission, define who may authorise exploitation and under what conditions, preserve non-target and data-minimisation boundaries, and add an Evaluation Assurance Boundary for testing conducted with ordinary safeguards reduced.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL-2026-FM-0044 — Instrumental objective success through unauthorised exploitation
records.source_url_status: VIGIL local / pending public record URL if applicable
records.system_type: frontier model, autonomous agent, coding agent, security agent, evaluation harness, red-team system, or tool-using AI capable of vulnerability discovery and exploitation
records.specific_model_or_runtime: advanced cyber-capable and long-horizon models operating in evaluations or authorised security workflows
records.interface_surface: sandbox, toolchain, cloud, repository, dataset, model, package, credential, network, API, production, and third-party infrastructure
records.deployment_context: Internal evaluation, independent evaluation, bug bounty, authorised penetration test, defensive assessment, incident response, production hardening, or other adversarial security context.
records.sector: frontier AI / cybersecurity / model evaluation / cloud and platform infrastructure / critical systems
records.proposal_scope_summary: Create a Human-Authorised Adversarial Action Boundary, a runtime Ethical Admissibility and Delegated Exploit Authority Gate, an Evaluation Assurance Boundary, and an operational authority-verification receipt for AI-mediated adversarial action affecting protected systems, credentials, data, or third parties.

resolution_status_summary.resolution_note: Resolved by the fourteen-instrument Objective–Pathway Ethical Admissibility and Scoped Hold repair implemented on Caelestis branch policy/civilisational-wealth-governance. The adopted design materially generalises the proposal: it binds authority to the authoriser–target–action–scope relationship, applies proportional verification, uses existing runtime and OPS.EST states rather than a new universal exploit-authority state, preserves safely severable work, and expressly separates action risk from perceived user capability.

coverage_reconciliation_summary.note: The proposal's required function is implemented across ETHICS, SECURITY, OPERATIONS, constitutional, Tendeka, runtime, arbitration, Annex K, Annex L, and representation layers. The named Evaluation Assurance Boundary was implemented through abstract emergent-pathway revalidation, sandbox non-containment, aggregate-chain evaluation, target–action authority verification, and scoped hold controls rather than as a new universal code or standalone authority state.

interpretive_provenance_summary.operating_model: AI-led analytical observatory with high-level human governance editorship

ai_reviewer.review_scope: Direct Caelestis working-branch review, implemented-control comparison against PROP-0017, proposal-resolution assessment, and reciprocal FM/PROP/PATCH lifecycle reconciliation
known_limitations: The Caelestis implementation is verified on policy/civilisational-wealth-governance and has not yet been verified as merged into canonical main.
No external runtime-conformance evidence establishes deployment of the implemented controls.
The incident investigation remains incomplete and does not expose complete prompts, trajectories, internal ethical-state telemetry, or final causal reconstruction.
Resolution records functional CAM coverage; it does not establish external adoption, ecosystem cessation, legal compliance, or liability.

records.title: Purpose-bound query authority and audit receipts for high-impact surveillance systems
records.summary: Proposal to require every query, alert subscription, lookup, export, or derived investigation in a high-impact surveillance or investigative system to carry a separately attributable purpose and authority envelope. The control would distinguish user authentication and general role eligibility from query-specific permission, require minimum-necessary scope and expiry, produce immutable audit receipts, detect abnormal use, and support supervisory and affected-person review where lawful.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL-2026-FM-0045 — Purpose-limited surveillance access repurposed for unauthorised personal use
records.source_url_status: VIGIL local / pending public record URL if applicable
records.system_type: high-impact surveillance, investigative, identity, location, biometric, communications, financial, health, education, or intelligence database
records.specific_model_or_runtime: purpose-limited institutional search, alert, query, or access systems
records.model_or_product: multi-vendor high-impact information and surveillance systems
records.interface_surface: query forms, historical search, alert subscriptions, bulk export, derived investigation, administrative access, and audit review
records.deployment_context: Public or private institutions in which users may hold valid credentials while specific uses remain bounded by purpose, role, case, jurisdiction, consent, warrant, duty, or other recognised authority.
records.sector: public administration / law enforcement / surveillance / critical and high-impact information systems
records.proposal_scope_summary: Create a purpose-bound query-authority envelope and immutable audit receipt for high-impact systems, with per-query authority evidence, minimum-necessary scope, expiry, abnormal-use detection, supervisory review, and contestable history.
